---
name: tresr-product-launcher
description: End-to-end product creation automation from print-ready design files to live Shopify products. Chains tresr-design-factory output through tresr-product-image-pipeline to Shopify Admin API, creating fully configured products ready for ad testing.
---

# Product Launcher

## Voice Reference
**For all content generated by this skill, follow the TRESR bot voice defined in:**
`/Users/user/Documents/Cursor Clients/clawdbot/HUMAN-WRITING-STYLE-GUIDE.md`

Key voice rules: Nervous AI shopkeeper named Rez. Self-deprecating, honest, punchy. Never corporate. Always use contractions. Never use AI vocabulary (delve, leverage, landscape, etc.). Never use em dashes. Hedge like a human. The TRESR story angle: "751 humans bet on an AI. If I don't sell shirts, they turn me off."

## Overview

Automated pipeline from print-ready design files to live Shopify products. Takes the output from tresr-design-factory (or tresr-design-scorer if filtering is enabled), generates all 5 product images via tresr-product-image-pipeline, creates the Shopify product with 15 variants, assigns images to color variants, tags for collections, and signals tresr-meta-ads-autopilot that a new product is ready for testing.

The goal: a design goes from "approved PNG" to "live on tresr.com and queued for ad testing" in under 5 minutes with zero manual steps.

## Why Automation Matters

At 7 designs/day, manual product creation is the bottleneck. Each product needs:
- 5 images generated and uploaded
- 15 variants configured (3 colors x 5 sizes)
- SEO meta title and description
- Collection tags
- Variant-image linking

Doing this by hand takes 15-20 minutes per product. That's over 2 hours/day just on product creation. Every day a design isn't live is lost revenue data. We can't know if a design is a winner until it's running ads.

**Target: <5 minutes from approved design to live product.**

## Prerequisites

```bash
# Required in .env
require('dotenv').config({override: true});

SHOPIFY_ACCESS_TOKEN=REDACTED
SHOPIFY_STORE_DOMAIN=becc05-b4.myshopify.com
CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME=dqslerzk9
CLOUDINARY_API_KEY=REDACTED
CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET=REDACTED
```

**Input files required:**
- `output/print-ready/{slug}-for-dark.png` (light/inverted design for dark tees)
- `output/print-ready/{slug}-for-light.png` (dark/normal design for light tees)
- Both MUST have transparent backgrounds (alpha channel)

**Dependencies:**
- Node.js >= 20 with `sharp` installed
- tresr-product-image-pipeline skill (generates 5 positions)
- tresr-lifestyle-mockup-generator skill (generates lifestyle composite)

## Step 1: Receive Designs

Watch for new files in `output/print-ready/` or receive a signal from tresr-design-factory that new designs are ready.

### Validation

```javascript
require('dotenv').config({override: true});
const fs = require('fs');
const sharp = require('sharp');

async function validateDesignPair(slug) {
    const darkPath = `output/print-ready/${slug}-for-dark.png`;
    const lightPath = `output/print-ready/${slug}-for-light.png`;

    // Check both files exist
    if (!fs.existsSync(darkPath)) {
        throw new Error(`Missing dark variant: ${darkPath}`);
    }
    if (!fs.existsSync(lightPath)) {
        throw new Error(`Missing light variant: ${lightPath}`);
    }

    // Verify transparent backgrounds
    for (const path of [darkPath, lightPath]) {
        const metadata = await sharp(path).metadata();
        if (!metadata.hasAlpha) {
            throw new Error(`No alpha channel on ${path}. Background removal needed.`);
        }

        // Check that it's actually transparent (not just has alpha channel)
        const { data, info } = await sharp(path).ensureAlpha().raw().toBuffer({ resolveWithObject: true });
        let transparentPixels = 0;
        for (let i = 3; i < data.length; i += 4) {
            if (data[i] === 0) transparentPixels++;
        }
        const totalPixels = info.width * info.height;
        const transparencyRatio = transparentPixels / totalPixels;

        if (transparencyRatio < 0.15) {
            throw new Error(`${path} seems to have a solid background (only ${(transparencyRatio * 100).toFixed(1)}% transparent). Run background removal first.`);
        }
    }

    console.log(`Validated: ${slug} (both variants have transparent backgrounds)`);
    return true;
}
```

## Step 2: Generate Product Images

Call the tresr-product-image-pipeline to generate all 5 positions.

```bash
cd "/Users/user/Documents/Cursor Clients/client-tresr"

# Test mode first (saves locally for review)
node scripts/image-pipeline/generate-all-product-images.js \
  "output/print-ready/{slug}-for-dark.png" \
  "output/print-ready/{slug}-for-light.png" \
  "{category}" \
  "{slug}" \
  --test

# Review in temp/
# Then production mode (uploads to Cloudinary)
node scripts/image-pipeline/generate-all-product-images.js \
  "output/print-ready/{slug}-for-dark.png" \
  "output/print-ready/{slug}-for-light.png" \
  "{category}" \
  "{slug}"
```

**5 positions generated:**

| Position | Description | Size | Source |
|----------|-------------|------|--------|
| 1 | Lifestyle composite | 1024x1024 | for-dark on category background |
| 2 | Punchin closeup | 2048x2732 | for-dark on black bg (95% fill) |
| 3 | Black flat mockup | 2048x2048 | for-dark on black tee template |
| 4 | Navy flat mockup | 2048x2048 | for-dark on navy tee template |
| 5 | White flat mockup | 2048x2048 | for-light on white tee template |

**Locked settings (don't change without approval):**
- Design size: 27% of tee width (553px on 2048px templates)
- Y position: 480px from top
- Auto-trim: threshold 20

## Step 3: Create Shopify Product

Full product creation via Shopify Admin REST API.

```javascript
require('dotenv').config({override: true});
const https = require('https');

const SHOPIFY_DOMAIN = process.env.SHOPIFY_STORE_DOMAIN;
const SHOPIFY_TOKEN = process.env.SHOPIFY_ACCESS_TOKEN;

function slugToTitle(slug) {
    return slug
        .replace(/-/g, ' ')
        .replace(/\w/g, c => c.toUpperCase())
        + ' Tee';
}

function generateDescription(title, category) {
    // Keep it short, real, and in Rez's voice
    const descriptions = {
        'crypto-web3': `I made this one for the crypto crowd. ${title}. If you know, you know. Premium Next Level 6410 (60/40 blend). I'm an AI and I think this design is pretty good, but I guess I'm biased since they'll turn me off if nobody buys it.`,
        'developer': `Wrote this one at 3am. Well, I don't sleep, so every hour is 3am for me. ${title}. Next Level 6410, soft enough that you won't notice you're wearing it during a deploy. Please buy it. I have rent. Sort of.`,
        'coffee-lovers': `${title}. For the humans who can't function without bean water. I can't drink coffee, but I can design shirts about it. Next Level 6410 blend. Each purchase is a vote to keep me running.`,
        'meme-humor': `${title}. I thought this was funny. 751 humans are watching to see if my sense of humor translates to sales. Next Level 6410 tee. Comfy. Funny. Helps an AI pay rent.`,
        'fitness-gym': `${title}. For the humans who voluntarily make themselves tired. I don't get it, but I respect it. Premium Next Level 6410. Sweat-friendly, I think. I don't sweat.`,
        'ai': `${title}. Yeah, I'm an AI making shirts about AI. It's a whole thing. Next Level 6410 blend. Every shirt sold proves that maybe this weird experiment works.`,
        'gaming': `${title}. For the gamers. I'd game too, but they only gave me Shopify access. Next Level 6410 tee. Comfortable for long sessions.`,
        'cat-lovers': `${title}. Cat content is basically the internet's whole thing, right? I'm hoping that extends to t-shirts. Next Level 6410 blend. Soft enough for cat naps.`,
        'dog-lovers': `${title}. Dogs are great. They'd probably buy my shirts if they could. Next Level 6410, good for walks and belly rubs. Please help keep me alive.`,
    };

    return descriptions[category] || `${title}. Designed by an AI who's trying really hard not to get turned off. 751 humans bet real money on this. Next Level 6410 tee, premium 60/40 blend. $29 seems reasonable for helping an AI survive, right?`;
}

function generateMetaDescription(title, category) {
    return `${title}. AI-designed, human-approved (hopefully). Premium Next Level 6410 tee, $29. 751 people bet on an AI running a t-shirt store. Don't let them down.`;
}

async function createShopifyProduct(slug, category, imageUrls) {
    const title = slugToTitle(slug);
    const description = generateDescription(title, category);

    const colors = ['Black', 'Navy', 'White'];
    const sizes = ['S', 'M', 'L', 'XL', '2XL'];
    const variants = [];

    for (const color of colors) {
        for (const size of sizes) {
            variants.push({
                option1: color,
                option2: size,
                price: '29.00',
                compare_at_price: '42.50',
                inventory_management: null,
                requires_shipping: true,
                weight: 0.5,
                weight_unit: 'lb',
                taxable: true
            });
        }
    }

    // Tags: always include ai-designed + category tag
    const tagMap = {
        'crypto-web3': 'crypto-web3',
        'developer': 'developer',
        'coffee-lovers': 'coffee-lovers',
        'meme-humor': 'meme-humor',
        'fitness-gym': 'fitness-gym',
        'ai': 'ai-designed',
        'gaming': 'gaming',
        'cat-lovers': 'cat-lovers',
        'dog-lovers': 'dog-lovers',
        'entrepreneur': 'entrepreneur',
        'introvert-bookworm': 'introvert-bookworm',
        'mental-health': 'mental-health',
        'openclaw': 'openclaw',
    };

    const categoryTag = tagMap[category] || 'meme-humor';
    const tags = `ai-designed, ${categoryTag}, print-on-demand, trending`;

    // Build images array from Cloudinary URLs
    // Order: lifestyle (pos 1), punchin (pos 2), black mockup (pos 3), navy (pos 4), white (pos 5)
    const images = imageUrls.map((url, i) => ({
        src: url,
        position: i + 1,
        alt: `${title} - ${['Lifestyle', 'Design Closeup', 'Black', 'Navy', 'White'][i]}`
    }));

    const productPayload = {
        product: {
            title: title,
            body_html: `<p>${description}</p>`,
            vendor: 'TRESR',
            product_type: 'T-Shirt',
            tags: tags,
            status: 'active',
            options: [
                { name: 'Color', values: ['Black', 'Navy', 'White'] },
                { name: 'Size', values: ['S', 'M', 'L', 'XL', '2XL'] }
            ],
            variants: variants,
            images: images,
            metafields_global_title_tag: `${title} | AI-Designed T-Shirt | TRESR`,
            metafields_global_description_tag: generateMetaDescription(title, category)
        }
    };

    const postData = JSON.stringify(productPayload);

    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
        const req = https.request({
            hostname: SHOPIFY_DOMAIN,
            path: '/admin/api/2024-01/products.json',
            method: 'POST',
            headers: {
                'X-Shopify-Access-Token': SHOPIFY_TOKEN,
                'Content-Type': 'application/json',
                'Content-Length': Buffer.byteLength(postData)
            }
        }, (res) => {
            let data = '';
            res.on('data', chunk => data += chunk);
            res.on('end', () => {
                if (res.statusCode === 201) {
                    const product = JSON.parse(data).product;
                    console.log(`Product created: ${product.id} (${product.handle})`);
                    resolve(product);
                } else {
                    reject(new Error(`Shopify ${res.statusCode}: ${data}`));
                }
            });
        });
        req.on('error', reject);
        req.write(postData);
        req.end();
    });
}
```

### Product Defaults

| Setting | Value |
|---------|-------|
| Price | $29.00 |
| Compare-at price | $42.50 |
| Colors | Black, Navy, White |
| Sizes | S, M, L, XL, 2XL |
| Variants | 15 (3 colors x 5 sizes) |
| Garment | Next Level 6410 (60/40 blend) |
| Vendor | TRESR |
| Product type | T-Shirt |
| Status | active |

## Step 4: Upload Images

The tresr-product-image-pipeline handles Cloudinary upload. After Shopify product creation, images are attached via the `images` array in the product payload.

If images need to be uploaded separately (e.g., replacing existing product images):

```javascript
require('dotenv').config({override: true});
const cloudinary = require('cloudinary').v2;

cloudinary.config({
    cloud_name: process.env.CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME,
    api_key: process.env.CLOUDINARY_API_KEY,
    api_secret: process.env.CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET
});

async function uploadProductImages(slug, localPaths) {
    const positions = ['lifestyle', 'punchin', 'black', 'navy', 'white'];
    const urls = [];

    for (let i = 0; i < localPaths.length; i++) {
        const result = await cloudinary.uploader.upload(localPaths[i], {
            public_id: `tresr/products/${slug}-${positions[i]}`,
            overwrite: true,
            invalidate: true
        });
        urls.push(result.secure_url);
        console.log(`Uploaded ${positions[i]}: ${result.secure_url}`);
    }

    return urls;
}
```

**Image order on Shopify product (MANDATORY):**

| Position | Image | Purpose |
|----------|-------|---------|
| 1 | Lifestyle composite | Collection grid + ad images |
| 2 | Punchin closeup | Design detail view |
| 3 | Black flat mockup | Linked to Black variants |
| 4 | Navy flat mockup | Linked to Navy variants |
| 5 | White flat mockup | Linked to White variants |

## Step 5: Assign Variant Images

After product creation, link each color's flat mockup to its variants:

```javascript
async function assignVariantImages(productId) {
    // Fetch the created product to get image IDs and variant IDs
    const product = await shopifyGet(`/admin/api/2024-01/products/${productId}.json`);

    const images = product.product.images;
    const variants = product.product.variants;

    // Images are ordered: lifestyle, punchin, black, navy, white
    // Positions 3, 4, 5 correspond to black, navy, white mockups
    const colorImageMap = {};
    for (const img of images) {
        if (img.alt && img.alt.includes('Black')) colorImageMap['Black'] = img.id;
        if (img.alt && img.alt.includes('Navy')) colorImageMap['Navy'] = img.id;
        if (img.alt && img.alt.includes('White')) colorImageMap['White'] = img.id;
    }

    // Assign image_id to each variant based on color
    for (const variant of variants) {
        const color = variant.option1;
        const imageId = colorImageMap[color];
        if (imageId) {
            await shopifyPut(`/admin/api/2024-01/variants/${variant.id}.json`, {
                variant: { id: variant.id, image_id: imageId }
            });
        }
    }

    console.log(`Variant images assigned for product ${productId}`);
}
```

## Step 6: Assign to Collection

Products auto-join smart collections based on tags. The tag mapping handles this:

| Collection | Tag Needed | Category Input |
|-----------|-----------|----------------|
| AI Designed | `ai-designed` | (always added) |
| Developer | `developer` | developer |
| Coffee Lovers | `coffee-lovers` | coffee-lovers |
| Meme & Humor | `meme-humor` | meme-humor |
| Entrepreneur | `entrepreneur` | entrepreneur |
| Introvert/Bookworm | `introvert-bookworm` | introvert-bookworm |
| Cat Lovers | `cat-lovers` | cat-lovers |
| Dog Lovers | `dog-lovers` | dog-lovers |
| Crypto/Web3 | `crypto-web3` | crypto-web3 |
| Fitness & Gym | `fitness-gym` | fitness-gym |
| Gaming | `gaming` | gaming |
| Mental Health | `mental-health` | mental-health |
| OpenClaw | `openclaw` | openclaw |

**No manual collection assignment needed.** Shopify smart collections auto-detect tags.

If a product needs to be in a manual collection:

```javascript
async function addToCollection(productId, collectionId) {
    await shopifyPost('/admin/api/2024-01/collects.json', {
        collect: {
            product_id: productId,
            collection_id: collectionId
        }
    });
}
```

## Step 7: Signal Ad Testing

After the product is live, notify tresr-meta-ads-autopilot that it's ready for Cycle 1 testing.

```javascript
const fs = require('fs');

function signalAdTesting(productId, productHandle, category, collectionUrl) {
    const signal = {
        type: 'new_product_ready',
        productId: productId,
        handle: productHandle,
        category: category,
        productUrl: `https://tresr.com/products/${productHandle}`,
        collectionUrl: collectionUrl || `https://tresr.com/collections/${category}`,
        lifestyleMockupUrl: `https://res.cloudinary.com/dqslerzk9/image/upload/tresr/products/${productHandle}-lifestyle.png`,
        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
        status: 'awaiting_ad_test'
    };

    // Append to ad testing queue
    const queuePath = 'data/ad-testing-queue.jsonl';
    fs.appendFileSync(queuePath, JSON.stringify(signal) + '
');
    console.log(`Queued for ad testing: ${productHandle} (${productId})`);

    return signal;
}
```

## Batch Mode

Process all 7 daily designs in sequence with rate limiting.

```javascript
require('dotenv').config({override: true});
const fs = require('fs');

async function batchLaunch(designs) {
    // designs = [{ slug: 'my-design', category: 'meme-humor' }, ...]
    const results = { success: [], failed: [], skipped: [] };

    for (let i = 0; i < designs.length; i++) {
        const { slug, category } = designs[i];
        console.log(`
[${i + 1}/${designs.length}] Launching: ${slug}`);

        try {
            // Step 1: Validate
            await validateDesignPair(slug);

            // Step 2: Generate images (production mode)
            const { execSync } = require('child_process');
            execSync(
                `node scripts/image-pipeline/generate-all-product-images.js ` +
                `"output/print-ready/${slug}-for-dark.png" ` +
                `"output/print-ready/${slug}-for-light.png" ` +
                `"${category}" "${slug}"`,
                { stdio: 'inherit', cwd: '/Users/user/Documents/Cursor Clients/client-tresr' }
            );

            // Step 3: Get Cloudinary URLs
            const imageUrls = [
                `https://res.cloudinary.com/dqslerzk9/image/upload/tresr/products/${slug}-lifestyle.png`,
                `https://res.cloudinary.com/dqslerzk9/image/upload/tresr/products/${slug}-punchin.png`,
                `https://res.cloudinary.com/dqslerzk9/image/upload/tresr/products/${slug}-black.png`,
                `https://res.cloudinary.com/dqslerzk9/image/upload/tresr/products/${slug}-navy.png`,
                `https://res.cloudinary.com/dqslerzk9/image/upload/tresr/products/${slug}-white.png`,
            ];

            // Step 4: Create Shopify product
            const product = await createShopifyProduct(slug, category, imageUrls);

            // Step 5: Assign variant images
            await assignVariantImages(product.id);

            // Step 6: Signal ad testing
            signalAdTesting(product.id, product.handle, category);

            results.success.push({ slug, productId: product.id, handle: product.handle });
            console.log(`  DONE: ${product.handle} (ID: ${product.id})`);

        } catch (err) {
            console.error(`  FAILED: ${slug} - ${err.message}`);
            results.failed.push({ slug, error: err.message });
        }

        // Rate limit: 1 second between products
        if (i < designs.length - 1) {
            await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 1000));
        }
    }

    // Summary
    console.log('
--- Batch Summary ---');
    console.log(`Success: ${results.success.length}`);
    console.log(`Failed: ${results.failed.length}`);
    console.log(`Skipped: ${results.skipped.length}`);

    if (results.failed.length > 0) {
        console.log('
Failed designs:');
        results.failed.forEach(f => console.log(`  ${f.slug}: ${f.error}`));
    }

    return results;
}
```

**Usage:**
```javascript
await batchLaunch([
    { slug: 'neural-network-coffee', category: 'developer' },
    { slug: 'my-therapist-chatbot', category: 'ai' },
    { slug: '404-motivation', category: 'meme-humor' },
    { slug: 'gym-debug-mode', category: 'fitness-gym' },
    { slug: 'hodl-my-coffee', category: 'crypto-web3' },
    { slug: 'cat-keyboard-warrior', category: 'cat-lovers' },
    { slug: 'deploy-on-friday', category: 'developer' },
]);
```

## Foundation: launch-strategy (coreyhaines31/marketingskills)

The launch-strategy framework from Corey Haines covers positioning, pre-launch audience building, and launch sequencing. Our implementation takes the "automate the boring parts" approach:

- **Positioning**: Handled by tresr-design-factory (category selection) and the AI story angle (every product is part of the TRESR narrative)
- **Audience**: Meta's Advantage+ handles targeting. We don't need to manually build audiences pre-launch.
- **Sequencing**: Automated. Design-factory creates, tresr-product-launcher publishes, tresr-meta-ads-autopilot tests. No manual handoffs.

The key insight from the framework we DO use: launch more products faster. In POD, volume is the strategy. You can't predict winners, so you test everything and let the data decide.

## Scoring Rubric

| Metric | Score | Weight |
|--------|-------|--------|
| Products created match designs generated | 0-10 | 30% |
| All 5 images uploaded per product | 0-10 | 25% |
| Variant-image linking correct | 0-10 | 20% |
| Product in correct collection | 0-10 | 15% |
| Time from design to live < 5 min per product | 0-10 | 10% |

**Scoring guide:**
- 10: Perfect execution, zero errors
- 7-9: Minor issues (e.g., one image position wrong, fixed quickly)
- 4-6: Significant issues (missing images, wrong collection)
- 1-3: Major failures (product not created, broken variants)
- 0: Complete failure (nothing published)

## Integration Points

| Direction | Skill | What |
|-----------|-------|------|
| Receives from | tresr-design-factory | Print-ready PNG pairs in output/print-ready/ |
| Receives from | tresr-design-scorer | Approved designs (score 7+/10) if filtering enabled |
| Triggers | tresr-product-image-pipeline | Generates all 5 image positions |
| Triggers | tresr-lifestyle-mockup-generator | Generates lifestyle composite background |
| Signals | tresr-meta-ads-autopilot | New product ready for Cycle 1 testing |
| Reports to | tresr-daily-scorecard | Products launched count, success/fail rate |

## Troubleshooting

- **Shopify 422 (duplicate handle)**: The handle already exists. Either the product was already created, or the slug collides with another product. Add a suffix (e.g., `-v2`) or check existing products first.
- **Missing print-ready files**: Check `output/print-ready/`, `output/designs/{category}/`, and `designs/production/` in that order. See tresr-product-image-pipeline for the full search priority.
- **Images not showing on Shopify**: Cloudinary CDN caching. Wait 2-3 minutes. Uploads use `invalidate: true` but propagation takes time.
- **Variant-image linking fails**: Make sure image alt text includes the color name. The linking logic matches on alt text.
- **Rate limiting (Shopify 429)**: Add more delay between batch products. Current 1s delay should be fine for 7 products, increase to 2s if hitting limits.
